top_issues.message: Function 'liquidate' writes to storage after an external call — reentrancy risk
top_issues.suggestion: Move state updates before the external call, or use a reentrancy guard

top_issues.message: Function 'borrow' makes an external call and shares storage key 'v1_borrowed' with another externally-calling function — cross-function reentrancy risk
top_issues.suggestion: Use a shared reentrancy guard across functions that touch this key

top_issues.message: Function 'borrow' makes an external call and shares storage key 'v1_collateral' with another externally-calling function — cross-function reentrancy risk

findings.suggestion: Consider adding a reentrancy guard (e.g. a REENTRANCY_GUARD storage flag)

findings.message: Function 'liquidate' makes an external call and shares storage key 'v1_collateral' with another externally-calling function — cross-function reentrancy risk
findings.suggestion: Use a shared reentrancy guard across functions that touch this key

findings.message: Function 'borrow' performs unchecked 'total_borrowed + amount' on a financial type — possible overflow/underflow
findings.suggestion: Use total_borrowed.checked_add(amount) instead of the bare operator

findings.message: Function 'liquidate' modifies state without authorization check
findings.suggestion: Add require_auth() with the appropriate Address at the start of this function

findings.message: Function 'liquidate' takes Address parameter 'caller' but doesn't authenticate it
findings.suggestion: Use require_auth() with the Address parameter to ensure only that user can call

findings.message: Storage key 'v1_borrowed' written with different value types: '(total_borrowed + amount)' in 'borrow' and '0i128' in 'liquidate'
findings.suggestion: Use the same Rust type for all writes to this key, or rename one of the keys
